creator: fbsd-send-pr-200903@lackaday.org
text:
After the 1st report, I looked more a bit.
----
CONFIGURE_TARGET=${MACHINE_ARCH}-portbld-freeBSD${OSREL}
CONFIGURE_ARGS+= --host=${ARCH}-portbld-freeBSD${OSREL} \
                 --target=${ARCH}-portbld-freeBSD${OSREL} \
                 ...
----
would be better.
The source of problem looks like confusion/comprication on naming/design 
mostly in bsd.port.mk.
Many ports including(but not limited to) lang/gcc43 will be affected indeed.
If anyone build package(ports) in cross,
(e.g. Building "packages for FreeBSD/sun4v" on FreeBSD/amd64 box.)
then the problem will pop up although it's lying off while the native building.
I filed it as a separate PR to keep this to be closed, see PR: ports/132783.


As just a comment (mean not request):
From viewpoint of an application/library (mean NOT FreeBSD and/or GCC itself) 
developper in C (mean NOT C++),
it can be helpful if various versions of complilers are available.
Even they are old versions. Indeed, even gcc2 can be meaningful.
In different from C++, C is a solid/stable already in language specification.
The GCC project tends to dropping and dropping architecture support.
So, 
 * An application developper (call (s)he as Dev-A) works on 
   an application (call it as App-B) which is written in C.
 * Dev-A is working with recent gcc u.v.w on an FreeBSD/i386 box.
 * An user (call (s)he as User-D) want to use App-B 
   but the current gcc does not support his/her Anything/dropped box.
   The last supported is x.y.z.
then, Dev-A can work easily a bit if gcc-x.y.z is available on 
his FreeBSD/i386 box.

In more specific, there is a big gap in between 3.3.x and 3.4.x(or later).
It's the -f{input|exec}*-charset|-f-wide-exec-charset things.
The actual body of conversion is GNU libiconv.
The NORMAL version of GNU libiconv is welknown as un-usable for many Japanese
becauseof the CP932 related problem.
(So, ports/converters/libiconv has EXTRA_PATCHES option :-)
It's just a example. iconv related is trouble source.
Thus, gcc-3.3.6
  -- the last version BEFORE -f{input|exec}*-charset|-f-wide-exec-charset
     had introduced --
is a good point to fall back on for troubleshooting to isolate problems.

But I must agree that almost peeple would not be interested in 
these so old versions.
